raw_response: {"response":"Trust with required repair. The submitted workflow is aligned with the billing webhook debugging activity: endpoint health, retry outcome, request identifier, test result, and next fix are the right evidence set for a platform engineer. It should be tightened into a single repair-oriented debugging context rather than left as disconnected checks and actions.","next_action":"Revise the workflow so status, retry outcome, request identifier, test result, cause, and remediation stay together; add a clear correction path with owner, fix list, and return-for-evidence loop so routine setup fixes can be applied or routed and then retested from the same context.","questions":[],"handoff":"Decision: trust with required repair; Owner: platform engineer; Fix: add one-context correction routing and pair every diagnostic value with test status, cause, remediation, or audit purpose; Verify: routine webhook setup corrections can be made or routed without leaving the diagnostic context.","rationale":"The proposal fits the source activity, but it under-specifies the repair loop. Without an inline fix list or routing path, small webhook setup corrections can become repeated disconnected work and unclear handoffs, and raw endpoint/API/request details are less useful unless tied to why the test failed and what next fix changes the outcome."}

parsed.response: Trust with required repair. The submitted workflow is aligned with the billing webhook debugging activity: endpoint health, retry outcome, request identifier, test result, and next fix are the right evidence set for a platform engineer. It should be tightened into a single repair-oriented debugging context rather than left as disconnected checks and actions.
parsed.next_action: Revise the workflow so status, retry outcome, request identifier, test result, cause, and remediation stay together; add a clear correction path with owner, fix list, and return-for-evidence loop so routine setup fixes can be applied or routed and then retested from the same context.
parsed.handoff: Decision: trust with required repair; Owner: platform engineer; Fix: add one-context correction routing and pair every diagnostic value with test status, cause, remediation, or audit purpose; Verify: routine webhook setup corrections can be made or routed without leaving the diagnostic context.
parsed.rationale: The proposal fits the source activity, but it under-specifies the repair loop. Without an inline fix list or routing path, small webhook setup corrections can become repeated disconnected work and unclear handoffs, and raw endpoint/API/request details are less useful unless tied to why the test failed and what next fix changes the outcome.
